methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cuby Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cover is a Series B-stage housing and construction technology company founded by Alexis Rivas and Jemuel Joseph. The target has raised $100M+ to date. The Housing & Construction sector has limited public comparables (0 identified) and only 2 private peers (ICON and Cuby Technologies), neither of which provided sufficient data density for reliable peer median analysis.

Given the sparse comp universe, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using the $100M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $190M. This multiplier reflects the risk-adjusted return expectations between Series B and subsequent financing stages within the sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, with a median point of $190M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the actual quality and growth trajectory of Cover relative to the limited peer set, and whether the 1.9x multiplier appropriately captures current market conditions in frontier construction technology. The single-method triangulation also limits confidence; stronger conviction would require either (1) additional same-stage Housing & Construction comparables or (2) revenue/growth multiples if sufficient operating data were available.
